sections.heading: Essential Eight explained
sections.summary: The Essential Eight is a set of eight prioritised cyber security mitigation strategies developed by the Australian Signals Directorate (ASD) to protect organisations' internet-connected information technology networks from cyber threats. These strategies include patching applications and operating systems, multi-factor authentication, restricting administrative privileges, application control, restricting Microsoft Office macros, user application hardening, and regular backups. The Essential Eight maturity model provides a framework for implementing these strategies based on ASD's cyber threat intelligence and incident response experience.

requirements.text: The Essential Eight has been designed to protect organisations' internet-connected information technology networks.
The Essential Eight maturity model supports the implementation of the Essential Eight.

sections.source_quote: The Essential Eight has been designed to protect organisations' internet-connected information technology networks.

sections.heading: Essential Eight explained
sections.summary: Organisations should conduct assessments against the Essential Eight using the Essential Eight assessment process guide. Additional information on the Essential Eight maturity model and implementation is available through the Essential Eight maturity model FAQ publication. Contact details for inquiries include a phone number (1300 CYBER1) and email (cyber.gov.au).

requirements.text: Assessments against the Essential Eight should be conducted using the Essential Eight assessment process guide.

sections.source_quote: Assessments against the Essential Eight should be conducted using the Essential Eight assessment process guide.

sections.heading: Disclaimer

requirements.text: The material in this guide is of a general nature and should not be regarded as legal advice or relied on for assistance in any particular circumstance or emergency situation.
